BEIT LAHIA, Gaza Strip — An Israeli drone buzzes over a sea of debris in northern Gaza, where homes were turned into mass graves. original.title: Hamas says a son of its chief negotiator was killed in an Israeli strike on Gaza

signals.page_title: Hamas says a son of its chief negotiator was killed in an Israeli strike on Gaza - Los Angeles Times
signals.text_snippet: Sections LOG IN Show Search ADVERTISEMENT WORLD & NATION Hamas says a son of its chief negotiator was killed in an Israeli strike on Gaza Mourners carry the body of Azzam al-Hayya, son of Hamas leader Khalil al-Hayya, after he was killed in an Israeli airstrike, at Shifa Hospital in Gaza City Thursday. (Jehad Alshrafi / Associated Press) By Wafaa Shurafa and Toqa Ezzidin May 7, 2026 9:38 AM PT 3 5 min Click here to listen to this article Hamas says an Israeli strike killed the 32-year-old son of its chief negotiator in Gaza City, with the group accusing Israel of targeting family members to pressure negotiators. Israel’s continued strikes coincide with a U.S.-brokered ceasefire that was supposed to halt major military operations, though critical provisions—including Hamas disarmament and Israeli troop withdrawal—remain unmet. More than 72,000 Palestinians have died in Gaza since the war
